_comment: S7 Compounding / co-channel overlap — recovery under signals that physically sum in one slot at a single receiver (STUDY-SPEC §6). Each part lists explicit per-signal (msg_id, freq_hz, dt_s, snr_db). The harness renders each station clean, scales it by its relative snr_db (so snr_db sets station STRENGTH — capture pairs really differ in level), sums them, and adds ONE shared band-noise floor. Truth is logged one row PER SIGNAL so the existing matcher scores each compounded message independently. 2026-06-19: All exactly-co-frequency (Δ0 Hz) parts revised to use realistic ~10 Hz frequency offsets — in practice two operators independently targeting the same waterfall channel differ by several Hz due to display resolution and click precision. Exact co-frequency (Δ0 Hz) is a theoretical bound not representative of on-air conditions. Historical S7 results prior to this revision used Δ0 Hz co_channel parts and are not directly comparable to runs using this scenario.
name: Compounding / co-channel overlap
response: Decoded / not-decoded per compounded message (attribute, per-signal recovery)
description: 4 overlap families × 3 trials. Drives: per-message recovery and between-app agreement when 2–3 signals occupy the same or near-same audio frequency / time slot — the real-world pileup case that S4 (frequency-spread QRM) never exercises. Revised 2026-06-19: co_channel, time_freq and capture families now use realistic ~10 Hz inter-signal frequency offsets rather than exact co-frequency (Δ0 Hz), which does not occur in practice.
harness_note: Rendered by _render_compound via channel.mix_to_shared_floor: each signal is encoded clean, scaled by 10^(snr_db/20) (relative strength vs the shared floor), summed, then a single seeded AWGN floor is added once for the whole slot (NOT one floor per signal). One truth row is written per signal, carrying that signal's true freq/dt/snr (snr = strength vs the shared floor), so the matcher's text+freq branch scores each message on its own.

parts.label: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ10 Hz
parts.note: Two stations targeting the same channel with realistic ~10 Hz tuning offset — representative of real-world co-channel pileup where operators independently click approximately the same waterfall spot. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: 2-stack, equal -5 dB, Δ10 Hz
parts.note: Co-channel pair with realistic frequency offset near the decode floor. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: 3-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ10 Hz spread
parts.note: Triple co-channel pileup with realistic per-station frequency offsets — three operators independently targeting ~1500 Hz with natural waterfall tuning spread. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: delta 3 Hz
parts.note: Sub-bin separation (< one 6.25 Hz tone bin).

parts.label: delta 50 Hz
parts.note: Edge-to-edge — one full FT8 bandwidth apart; expected to separate cleanly.

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, dt 0.0 / 0.5 s
parts.note: Near-co-frequency pair with realistic 10 Hz offset, late replier offset by 0.5 s. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, dt 0.0 / 1.0 s
parts.note: Near-co-frequency pair with realistic 10 Hz offset, 1.0 s time stagger. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, dt 0.0 / 2.0 s
parts.note: Near-co-frequency pair with realistic 10 Hz offset, 2.0 s time stagger (near sync-window edge). Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, 0 / -3 dB
parts.note: Capture effect: 3 dB stronger vs weaker, near-co-frequency with realistic 10 Hz offset. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, 0 / -6 dB
parts.note: 6 dB capture margin with realistic 10 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, 0 / -10 dB
parts.note: 10 dB capture margin — weaker likely suppressed; realistic 10 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ10 Hz, +3 / -10 dB
parts.note: Strong dominant signal over a weak one; realistic 10 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ0 Hz (2026-06-19).

trials_note: Increased from 3 to 10 on 2026-06-19 to improve statistical resolution for the revised co_channel parts (Δ10 Hz). At K=10 the co_channel family yields ~70 binary observations, giving a minimum detectable improvement of ~9% at α=0.05 and per-part resolution of ±15%. May be reduced back to 3 for routine regression gates once a stable baseline has been established under the revised scenario.
